---
title: "Pie Chart"
---

The `.pie` style renders items as equal-sized wedges of a circle. Like all styles in this extension, it is activated via `filters: [diagrams]`.

## Basic usage

````markdown
::: pie
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::
````

::: pie
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::

## Donut

Add the `.donut` modifier to render the pie with a hollow center. All other
pie features — per-slice `color=`, `node-color=`, and `.gap` — work the same way.

````markdown
::: {.pie .donut}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
:::
````

::: {.pie .donut}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
:::

### Hole size

Control the ring thickness with `hole=`, given as a fraction of the radius
(default `0.55`, clamped between `0.2` and `0.85`). A larger value means a
bigger hole and a thinner ring.

````markdown
::: {.pie .donut hole="0.7"}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::
````

::: {.pie .donut hole="0.7"}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::

### Center text

Use `center=` to place a label or figure in the middle of the donut.

````markdown
::: {.pie .donut center="100%"}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
:::
````

::: {.pie .donut center="100%"}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
:::

## Gap between slices

Add the `.gap` modifier to offset all slices outward, creating visible separation.

````markdown
::: {.pie .gap}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::
````

::: {.pie .gap}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::

## Highlighting individual slices

Add `.gap` to specific items to pull those slices out while the rest stay in place.

````markdown
::: pie
::: {.item .gap}
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: {.item .gap}
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::
````

::: pie
::: {.item .gap}
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: {.item .gap}
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::

## Colors

Use `node-color=` on the container to set a shared color, or `color=` on individual items to override per slice.

````markdown
::: {.pie node-color="#e67e22"}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::
````

::: {.pie node-color="#e67e22"}
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::

````markdown
::: pie
::: {.item color="#e74c3c"}
Alice
:::
::: {.item color="#e67e22"}
Bob
:::
::: {.item color="#27ae60"}
Carol
:::
::: {.item color="#2980b9"}
David
:::
::: {.item color="#8e44ad"}
Eve
:::
:::
````

::: pie
::: {.item color="#e74c3c"}
Alice
:::
::: {.item color="#e67e22"}
Bob
:::
::: {.item color="#27ae60"}
Carol
:::
::: {.item color="#2980b9"}
David
:::
::: {.item color="#8e44ad"}
Eve
:::
:::
